title: Infrastructure Engineer
description:
**about us**
------------



drivewealth is on a mission to make investing easier. we believe that everyone should have the ability to control their financial future, and that access to financial markets should not be limited by geography, wealth, or legacy systems. we are a global b2b financial technology organization dedicated to democratizing access to financial independence around the world. our mission is realized through an api\-based platform, empowering our partners to offer seamless investing and trading experiences to clients worldwide, all from their mobile devices. our technology provides partners with a modern, extensible toolkit, enabling traditional investment workflows and innovative techniques like fractional share ownership. drivewealth has evolved into a global platform offering trading of us equities, mutual funds, etfs, fixed income, and options.



there’s never been a better time to build a category\-defining business and there has rarely been a team better positioned for this opportunity. our culture blends the pace and agility of a fintech start\-up with the impact, stability, and discipline of wall street. we encourage creativity and experimentation while ensuring institutional\-grade execution and regulatory compliance in everything we do. join us and help build the future of global investing!

**about the role**



as a seasoned infrastructure engineer, you will design, manage, and scale our high\-performance computing environments. this role bridges the gap between traditional system  

administration and modern platform engineering, focusing on reliability, automation, and the stringent security requirements of the financial services industry. you will be responsible for a  

hybrid ecosystem that spans aws cloud and on\-premise systems, ensuring 99\.99% uptime for mission\-critical services and transactions.


**what you’ll do**


* **linux systems engineering**: design and manage high\-performance linux environments including ubuntu, rhel, or amazon linux. focus on tuning for low\- latency financial applications and system\-level hardening
* **infrastructure as code (iac)**: architect and maintain scalable cloud infrastructure using **terraform and ansible**, treating infrastructure as a software project with version control  

and peer reviews
* **hybrid networking**: manage connectivity between cloud environments and on\-premise systems, focusing on secure vpc design, vpns, and routing
* **on\-premise management**: manage physical switches, routers, and firewalls, with an understanding of multicast, vlans, and interface aggregation
* **ci/cd \& release engineering**: build and optimize deployment pipelines to ensure system updates, patches, and application code are deployed safely and frequently
* **observability \& reliability**: implement comprehensive monitoring (using prometheus, loki, and grafana) to track system slis/slos and implement automated alerting or self\-  

healing scripts
* **container platform management**: support and scale docker / podman environments, focusing on pod networking and container runtimes
* **infrastructure security**: implement "shift\-left" security, including os\-level hardening (selinux, apparmor), automated vulnerability scanning, and iam policy refinement


**what you’ll need**


* **education**: bachelor’s degree in computer science, engineering, it, or a related field
* **systems expertise**: 3\+ years of experience in linux administration or infrastructure engineering
* **system internals**: deep knowledge of system performance profiling (e.g., top, iostat, strace) and comfort with linux system fundamentals (i.e. systemd, lvm, and cron)
* **automation proficiency**: strong proficiency in ansible and terraform for infrastructure and system automation. bash and/or python experience for custom tooling as required
* **cloud \& networking fundamentals**: hands\-on experience with aws networking fundamentals (vpcs, routing tables, and nat gateways). solid understanding of tcp/ip, dns, and routing protocols like bgp or ospf
* **container runtime**: experience with container runtimes in a production environment (docker / podman, docker compose). kubernetes experience is a plus
* **problem solving**: ability to perform root\-cause analysis on complex distributed systems  

  

applicants must be authorized to work for any employer in the u.s. drivewealth is unable to sponsor an employment visa at this time.


**special knowledge (nice to have, but not required)**


* **compliance knowledge**: experience working within regulated environments such as soc2, or finra
* **high\-availability architecture**: experience building multi\-region, failover\-capable architectures for mission\-critical financial transactions
* **sdlc architecture**: architecting deployment pipelines using github actions, familiarity with aws secrets manager for managing sensitive secrets, and other devops toolchain  

services
* **fintech protocols**: basic understanding of financial messaging or low\-latency requirements, such as fix protocol
* **security \& application tuning**: experience with waf, ddos mitigation strategies, and tuning edge application services such as haproxy
* **certifications**: comptia linux\+, rhcsa/rhce, or aws sysops administrator
**compensation**  

compensation package offerings are based on candidate experience and technical qualifications, as it relates to the role. these are identified and determined throughout your interviewing experience.  

  

***please note****: this role is expected to come into our office on a cadence set by the hiring manager/team.*


new york, ny (hybrid) pay range


$150,000 \- $180,000 usd

**working at drivewealth**
--------------------------



we do our best work when we’re in the same room. to maintain the speed our partners expect, our new york and chicago teams work in\-office 4 days a week. we’ve found that being physically side\-by\-side is the only way to solve complex problems in real\-time and stay truly accountable to the products we ship. when you’re here, you’re working directly with the people making the decisions. to support that work, we provide competitive compensation, equity, and a 401(k) match. we also offer full insurance coverage, a wellness reimbursement, a company\-provided phone, and a personal development allowance. finally, we value the time you spend away from the office with generous pto, observed holidays, and extended leave.


**work authorization**
----------------------



applicants must possess the legal right to work in the country where the position is located at the time of application. drivewealth requires all employees to provide original documentation verifying their work authorization on or before their first day of employment.



for us\-based roles: applicants must be currently authorized to work in the united states on a full\-time basis without the need for current or future visa sponsorship. drivewealth does not provide visa sponsorship or support for employment authorization, including transfers, at this time. offers of employment are strictly contingent upon an individual’s ability to secure and maintain the legal right to work at the company.


**how we think about ai**
-------------------------



we leverage ai to work smarter and move faster. we seek ai\-curious talent who are proactive about using emerging tools to increase signal quality, reduce friction, and improve outcomes to deliver products faster, provide better service to our partners, and to streamline processes. your ability to leverage our internal tools and technology to drive results is as important to us as your core domain expertise.


**compensation**
----------------



pay is generally based on the level, complexity, responsibility, location, and job duties/requirements of the specific position. we then source candidates with the requisite skills, expertise, education, training, and experience. if you are selected for an interview, please feel welcome to speak to a recruiter about our compensation philosophy and other available benefits. this role is eligible for base, bonus, equity, 401(k) match, and heavily subsidized benefits and perks.


**equal employment opportunity**
--------------------------------



to build technology and products that are used and loved by people and solve real\-world problems, we need to build a team with many different perspectives and experiences. we are an equal opportunity employer. we do not discriminate based on race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status. we encourage candidates from all backgrounds to apply.


**agency disclaimer**
---------------------



drivewealth does not accept agency resumes. do not forward resumes to our jobs alias, employees, or any other organization location. drivewealth is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

title: Senior Data Science Analyst
description:
**work schedule**


standard (mon\-fri)**environmental conditions**


office**job description**


when you join us at thermo fisher scientific, you’ll be part of an inquisitive team that shares your passion for exploration and discovery. with revenues of more than $40 billion and the largest investment in r\&d in the industry, we give our people the resources and chances to create significant contributions to the world.


if you are passionate about how the intersection of finance, data solutions, data science, analytics, and data visualization come together to tell a story and drive decision\-making within a world\-leading life science company, then this is the role for you!


part of the finance digital transformation organization, the **senior data science analyst** will be working closely across the entire matrix of divisions and functions. they will be responsible for design, development and maintenance of analytics and data solutions – with customers across all levels and technical backgrounds – all in service to produce data insights that drive overall efficiency, productivity, and revenue. the successful candidate will have strong business analytics and communication skills, combined with experience in data analytics and reporting tools, data etl, knowledge in modeling and excellent project management skills.

**key responsibilities:**

* apply data science techniques (statistical analysis, predictive modeling, experimentation) to uncover insights and drive decision\-making
* develop and maintain advanced dashboards and visualizations (e.g., power bi) leveraging well\-structured data models
* translate business requirements into robust advanced analytics and ai solutions, partnering closely with finance, operations, and it stakeholders
* drive continuous improvement initiatives, identifying opportunities for automation, efficiency gains, and cost savings
* explore and experiment with generative ai tools and workflows (e.g., code generation, data exploration, natural language querying, and automation) to enhance productivity and insight generation
* evaluate and pilot ai\-assisted analytics use cases, integrating them responsibly into existing data ecosystems where appropriate
* design, build, and maintain scalable data pipelines and analytics solutions using python and sql
* mentor and provide technical guidance to junior team members on coding standards, modeling best practices, and analytics methodologies

**requirements/qualifications:**

* bachelor’s degree in a quantitative field, such as statistics, computer science, mathematics, data science, finance, business administration, or a related field, master’s preferred
* 5\+ years’ experience in a reporting/data analyst or data scientist role with progressive responsibilities and scope
* experience building models and analyzing large, complex data sets yielding opportunities for revenue and/or process improvement within an organization
* technical proficiency in python and sql
* expertise in data reporting tools (power bi, tableau, sas analytics)
* interpersonal skills: exceptional verbal/written communication
* proven track record of achieving desired results without direct report authority
* knowledge of agile/scrum methodology is a plus


relocation assistance is not provided.


must be legally authorized to work in the united states now or in the future without sponsorship.


must be able to pass a comprehensive background check, which includes a drug screening.


our mission is to enable our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer. watch as our colleagues explain 5 reasons to work with us. as one team of 100,000\+ colleagues, we share a common set of values \- integrity, intensity, innovation and involvement \- working together to accelerate research, solve complex scientific challenges, drive technological innovation and support patients in need. \#startyourstory at thermo fisher scientific, where diverse experiences, backgrounds and perspectives are valued.

title: Senior Data Engineer (HYBRID)
description:
**senior data engineer**
========================

 **stack:** javascript (node/lambdas), python, sql, snowflake, cursor

**if you are unable to complete this application due to a disability, contact this employer to ask for accommodation or an alternative application process.**

**the mission**
---------------


we’re looking for a senior data engineer to serve as the foundational architect of our ai infrastructure. in this role, you will be a key pillar of the ai team, responsible for transforming raw, disparate data into scalable organizational intelligence. you aren’t just building pipelines; you are designing the enterprise\-grade framework that powers c\-4 analytics' digital marketing platforms, ensuring our data architecture is as innovative as the solutions it supports.

**the role**
------------


as a senior data engineer, you are expected to be a master of data orchestration. you are a self\-starter who navigates the complexities of snowflake, ai pipelines, and unstructured data environments with precision. you understand that your job is to connect the dots between raw sources and production\-ready solutions, implementing best practices in sharding, indexing, and normalization to optimize retrieval at scale. you’ll work across cross\-functional teams to align data architecture with business objectives, ensuring every system you design is elegant, documented, and mission\-critical.

### **key responsibilities:**

* **data architecture \& strategy:** lead by example in designing and maintaining high\-performance data systems. you aren’t just moving data; you are implementing best practices in sharding, replication, and indexing to ensure our database architecture is scalable, cost\-effective, and optimized for complex access patterns.
* **enterprise orchestration:** build and manage production\-ready ai pipelines that turn disparate, unstructured data into clean, normalized organizational intelligence. you bridge the gap between batch and streaming processes, connecting various sources to snowflake with precision and reliability.
* **ai\-driven innovation:** core requirement. you are a pioneer in the ai space, fully proficient in using ai development assistants to accelerate the transition from cutting\-edge proof\-of\-concepts to robust, production\-ready solutions. you leverage these tools to refactor data flows and maintain an elite standard of quality.
* **enablement \& collaboration:** craft intuitive tools and frameworks that empower our data scientists and analysts to do their best work. you act as a strategic partner to cross\-functional teams, ensuring our data storage and retrieval systems are perfectly aligned with broader business objectives.
* **documentation \& leadership:** act as the visionary for our information architecture. you design elegant, scalable solutions and document them with such clarity that others can easily follow your lead. you provide the technical guidance and architectural "blueprints" necessary for the team to grow and succeed.

**who you are**
---------------

* **the strategic architect:** you are fluent in python and sql, possessing the rare ability to express complex logic with elegant simplicity. you aren't just a coder; you are a database strategist who knows exactly when to deploy relational, vector, graph, or document data models, backed by a deep understanding of sharding, replication, and indexing.
* **cloud \& data expert:** you are a confident cloud navigator within the aws ecosystem and a master of snowflake. with over 5 years of professional experience in ai and machine learning, you have a proven track record of integrating llms and nlp tasks with enterprise data platforms. you understand the nuances of building for scale and the importance of high\-fidelity data wrangling.
* **infrastructure poet:** you treat infrastructure as clear, reproducible code. you are an advocate for information architecture best practices, data modeling, and rigorous data governance. you are proficient in the modern sdlc for ai projects, ensuring that every pipeline you design is robust, secure, and production\-ready.

title: Senior Software Engineer (C, C++, Linux, Rest API)
description:
**it's fun to work in a company where people truly believe in what they're doing!**

**job description summary:**

senior c/c\+\+ software engineer (p4\) with deep expertise in system‑level architecture, high‑performance development, and cross‑platform engineering across windows and linux. skilled in abstracting os‑specific functionality using qt to deliver portable, reliable, and scalable system components. strong sql expertise across major databases (sql server, oracle, postgresql). experienced in modernizing legacy systems, including porting windows applications to linux. hands‑on proficiency with ai code assistants and containerization, enabling increased developer efficiency and modern delivery workflows. experience with ibm i and ibm z platforms is desirable.
**essential duties and responsibilities:**
==========================================

* lead collaboration with product, architecture, and cross‑functional teams to translate complex business and end‑user requirements into robust technical designs and long‑term platform strategies.
* design, architect, and deliver large‑scale, enterprise‑grade software systems, ensuring performance, scalability, reliability, and maintainability.
* drive architectural discussions, define technical direction, and influence product roadmaps through informed recommendations on new features, system enhancements, and modernization initiatives.
* identify systemic code quality, performance, and architectural issues, and lead remediation through refactoring, redesign, or standardization activities.
* produce high‑quality technical documentation, design specifications, and architectural artifacts; communicate complex technical topics clearly to engineering and leadership stakeholders.
* serve as an escalation point for the most challenging debugging, production, and customer‑reported issues, providing guidance to other engineers and driving root‑cause resolution.
* operate with full autonomy to deliver complex enhancements, resolve critical defects, and implement major new product capabilities, often spanning multiple components or services.
* develop deep expertise in hardware–software interactions and system‑level integration, and provide architectural input on cross‑platform and cross‑component behaviors.
* demonstrate technical leadership in collaborative engineering practices, including leading code reviews, mentoring peers, setting coding standards, and improving development workflows.
* model a strong team‑oriented culture, driving accountability, ownership, and engineering excellence across the organization.
* champion continuous learning and domain mastery, proactively identifying opportunities to modernize tools, improve architecture, and elevate team capabilities.
* exhibit strategic curiosity and an innovation mindset, challenging assumptions, proposing new approaches, and influencing long‑term technical evolution.

**required qualifications:**

* 8\+ years of professional software engineering experience, with a proven record of delivering complex, high‑impact systems in production environments.
* demonstrated history of leading large‑scale, real‑world software development initiatives, including cross‑component or cross‑platform projects.
* expert‑level proficiency in c\+\+, with deep understanding of object‑oriented design, advanced language features, and architectural trade‑offs.
* strong understanding of rest apis, distributed system patterns, rdbms concepts, and sql, including query tuning and performance diagnostics on sql server, oracle, postgresql.
* extensive experience designing and developing enterprise‑grade applications, frameworks, or platform components.
* familiarity with parser and compiler‑adjacent tooling, such as antlr, javacc, yacc/bison, or custom parser frameworks—ability to analyze, modify, or extend grammar‑based systems.
* strong proficiency in both windows and linux environments, including command‑line tooling, system‑level troubleshooting, and cross‑platform development workflows.
* ability to mentor engineers, influence architectural decisions, and drive engineering best practices across teams.
* strong communication skills, capable of articulating complex technical topics to technical and non‑technical stakeholders.
* advanced expertise in **qt core** and other low\-level modules
* skilled at reducing platform‑specific code and increasing portability across windows and linux
* expert\-level experience in **both windows and linux** development
* strong command of os internals, filesystems, threading models, debugging tools
* **highly desirable:** leading major efforts to port windows applications to linux
* exposure to **ibm i (as/400\)** and/or **ibm z** architectures desirable

**ai \& developer productivity tools**

* hands\-on experience using ai code assistants such as:



	+ github copilot
	+ microsoft copilot
* ability to leverage ai tools for:



	+ code generation
	+ refactoring
	+ unit test creation
	+ documentation
	+ development acceleration
* strong understanding of validating and responsibly integrating ai\-generated code into production\-quality systems

**containerization \& devops**

* proficiency with **docker** for:



	+ image creation
	+ multi\-stage builds
	+ debugging containerized applications
	+ optimizing images for performance and footprint

**leadership \& senior\-level responsibilities**

* defines system architecture, drives design decisions, and sets engineering direction
* mentors junior and mid\-level engineers; leads deep technical reviews
* drives complex root\-cause analysis, performance investigations, and production issues
* partners with cross\-functional teams to align product direction with technical strategy
* owns long\-term, multi‑phase technical initiatives with autonomy
* models engineering excellence, code quality, and continuous improvement

title: Sr Analyst-Advanced Analytics & BI
description:
**job id** 10149937 **location** celebration, florida, united states **business** disney experiences **date posted** may 07, 2026
#### **job summary:**


**sr. analyst – advanced analytics \& business intelligence (bi)**

**about the role \& team**


“we create happiness.” that’s our motto at walt disney parks and resorts—and it drives everything we do. at disney, you’ll help push the limits of entertainment and deliver the never\-before\-seen. ready to make an impact?


the media growth and marketing team for disney experiences (dx), powers campaign activation. we lead the strategy and planning of audience\-focused, data\-driven communications across the offline (tv, radio, out\-of\-home), digital (display, social, search and streaming) and direct channels (email, direct mail and app push).


we're seeking a sr. analyst to drive advanced analytics for direct channel marketing. using machine learning and business intelligence, you will develop data\-driven insights and products to optimize audience targeting, messaging, and channels. you’ll collaborate with other planning and marketing teams to deliver actionable insights and recommend solutions.

**this roles sit in celebration, fl and reports to the sr manager – advanced analytics \& business intelligence (bi)**

**position responsibilities**


while each day will be different, with rotating priorities, the main themes for this role include you being a(n):

**data wrangler:** you understand data and how it supports solving business problems.

* you will translate the business and analytic needs into data requirements to ensure you have the information you need to build data pipelines for predictive models, actionable insights and operational dashboards.
* you will work in a large, complex data environment, compiling and cleansing data across a variety of domains to create a foundation for your analytic work.

**analytics creator:** you leverage data science skills to deliver predictive models, ad hoc statistical analyses and data visualizations.

* you will conduct end\-to\-end analysis that includes data collection and exploration, feature generation and reduction, and algorithm selection.
* you will determine the best techniques to use to solve problems efficiently and effectively, examining all aspects of the analytics curve across statistical, predictive, and prescriptive methods.
* you will design, develop, and deploy machine learning models (e.g., logistic regression, classification, decision trees, xgboost, lightgbm, clustering) to predict guest behavior including purchase likelihood, and engagement.
* you will develop intuitive, impactful dashboards and visual analytics using tools (e.g., tableau) to enable self\-service insights, monitor campaign metrics, and support data\-driven decision\-making across stakeholders.
* you will design experiments (a/b testing) to measure audience strategy effectiveness.

**insights translator:** you interpret outputs of analyses and models to make relevant recommendations.

* you will draw inferences, analyze kpis, guest behavior trends and identify limitations to accurately summarize your findings, relating them back to the problem at hand and drive business impact.
* you will successfully craft key takeaways and next steps in the context of business actionability, while facilitating the integration of your work into the strategic decision making process.
* you will mentor and lead data analytics best practices: guide peers by promoting code quality, model validation, reproducibility, and responsible ai practices including performance monitoring, bias mitigation, and stakeholder education.

**analytics engineer:** you productionalize your data, models and dashboards to make them available for marketing operations

* you will architect and deploy department data assets, including data pipelines, predictive models and data visualizations, using python, sql and cloud platforms (e.g. snowflake, dataiku)

**required qualifications**

* 3\-5\+ years of experience in customer\-focused analytics within industries such as hospitality, entertainment, or retail, including sales, marketing, and customer service functions
* 3\-5\+ years of hands\-on expertise in bi technologies, data visualization, a/b testing, and the application of machine learning algorithms such as linear regression, logistic regression, decision trees, and clustering
* strong understanding of relational databases, data pipelines, and exploratory data analysis (eda) using sql and python with large\-scale datasets
* experience with storytelling and presenting insights from quantitative information for non\-technical stakeholders
* proven ability to translate business questions into actionable analytical products
* time\-management skills, with ability to prioritize multiple requests and communicate status to partners/leadership
* critical thinking, attention to detail, and a curiosity\-driven mindset.

**technical skills**

* **required:** machine learning, sql, python or r
* **preferred:** snowflake, dataiku, tableau

**education:**

* **required:** bachelor’s degree in statistics, analytics, business, mathematics, computer science, marketing, psychology, or equivalent work experience
* **preferred:** master’s degree in same disciplines

**additional information:**

* this full\-time role is located in celebration, florida
* position will be in\-office monday – thursday, remote on friday
* as part of the interview process, there will be a technical skill assessment

**our benefits:** disney offers a rewards package to help you live your best life. this includes health and savings benefits, educational opportunities and special extras that only disney can provide. learn more about our benefits and perks at https://jobs.disneycareers.com/benefits  



  

the hiring range for this position in celebration, fl is $111,900\.00\-$150,000\.00 per year. the base pay actually offered will take into account internal equity and may vary depending on the candidate’s geographic region, job\-related knowledge, skills, and experience among other factors. a bonus and/or long\-term incentive units may be provided as part of the compensation package, in addition to the full range of medical, financial, and/or other benefits, depending on the level and position offered.

title: Engineer, Software Engineer - Entry Level - Java, Angular, or Python
description:
if you’re passionate about being part of a dynamic organization that enables a fortune 100 company with nearly $70 billion in annual sales to drive innovation and adopt new technologies that deliver business results, then nationwide’s technology team could be the place for you! at nationwide®, “on your side” goes beyond just words. our customers are at the center of everything we do and we’re looking for associates who are passionate about delivering extraordinary care.**join our commercial lines technology organization as an entry\-level software engineer and help build the next generation of intelligent solutions that power nationwide’s growing commercial insurance business. in this role, you will:**

* **collaborate with experienced engineers, architects, and business partners to design and build ai\-powered features that improve underwriting, pricing, and agent experiences.**
* **develop, test, and deploy production\-quality services and apis that integrate machine learning models into our current commercial lines technology platforms.**
* **work with real\-world insurance data, using tools like java, python, modern frameworks, and devsecops practices to turn ideas into scalable capabilities.**
* **learn and grow in a supportive environment with clear mentorship, hands\-on problem solving, and opportunities to contribute to high\-impact initiatives early in your career.**

**if you are curious, collaborative, and excited to apply ai to complex, real\-world business problems, we’d love to have you on the team.**

**additional highly preferred skills:**

* **proficiency in java, angular, or python**
* **hands on experience using claude code, github copilot agent mode, cursor, or similar agentic ai technologies**
* **clear communication**
* **sql**

**optional skills:**

**the following skills are a plus. you will develop this skillset in your first 6 months on the job if you don’t have it already.**

* **apigee**
* **kubernetes**
* **new relic**
* **github actions**
* **harness**
* **aws cloud**
* **splunk**

**this will be a hybrid role based out of the columbus, oh or des moines, ia office. the team comes int two days a week.**

**this role does not qualify for employer\-sponsored work authorization. nationwide does not participate in the stem opt extension program.**


\#li\-az1

**job description summary**


if you’re enthusiastic about delivering secure technology solutions to support a company providing extraordinary care to its customers, then nationwide technology is the place for you. nationwide's industry\-leading technology workforce embraces an agile work environment and a collaborative culture to deliver outstanding solutions and results. if that sounds like something you aspire to, we want to hear from you!  

  

as a software engineer, you will focus primarily on providing high quality, efficient technology solutions to business partners by crafting new software applications or modifying and/or supporting existing packaged or custom\-built applications. in this capacity, you’ll code, configure, test, debug, document and maintain applications.**job description**

**key responsibilities:**

* develops, tests, debugs, documents and deploys software according to standards and best practices.
* applies secure software and systems engineering practices throughout the delivery lifecycle to ensure our data and technology solutions are protected from threats and vulnerabilities.
* uses devsecops practices, tooling and techniques.
* adopts continuous improvement and learning.
* handles project priorities, deadlines, and deliverables proactively.


may perform other responsibilities as assigned.

**reporting relationships:** reports to a technology manager/director.

**typical skills and experiences:**

**education:** undergraduate studies in computer science, management information systems or other related degree. technology experience or non\-traditional technology certificates can be substituted for an undergraduate degree.

**experience:** one or more years of technology experience.

**knowledge, abilities and skills:** proficiency of at least one programming language such as java, c\+\+, python, javascript, etc. knowledge of software development lifecycle. good problem\-solving skills. ability to thrive in a team environment. insurance/financial services industry knowledge a plus.


other criteria, such as competencies and experiences, may take precedence.


staffing exceptions to the above must be approved by the director and hr officer.

**values:** regularly and consistently demonstrates nationwide values.

**job conditions:**

**overtime eligibility:** not eligible (exempt)

**working conditions:** office environment may vary based on team and/or location. associates may have on\-call responsibility after normal working hours and/or on weekends.

**ada:** the above statements cover what are generally believed to be principal and essential functions of this job. specific circumstances may allow or require some people assigned to the job to perform a somewhat different combination of duties.

**benefits**


we have an array of benefits to fit your needs, including: medical/dental/vision, life insurance, short and long term disability coverage, paid time off with newly hired associates receiving a minimum of 18 days paid time off each full calendar year pro\-rated quarterly based on hire date, nine paid holidays, 8 hours of lifetime paid time off, 8 hours of unity day paid time off, 401(k) with company match, company\-paid pension plan, business casual attire, and more. 

nationwide is an equal opportunity employer. we celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive culture where everyone feels challenged, appreciated, respected and engaged. nationwide prohibits discrimination and harassment and affords equal employment opportunities to employees and applicants without regard to any characteristic (or classification) protected by applicable law.


smoke\-free iowa statement: nationwide mutual insurance company, its affiliates and subsidiaries comply with the iowa smokefree air act. smoking is prohibited in all enclosed areas on or around company premises as well as company issued vehicles. the company offers designated smoking areas in which smoking is permitted at each individual location. the act prohibits retaliation for reporting complaints or violations. for more information on the iowa smokefree air act, individuals may contact the smokefree air act helpline at 888\-944\-2247\. **note to employment agencies:**


we value the partnerships we have built with our preferred vendors. nationwide does not accept unsolicited resumes from employment agencies. all resumes submitted by employment agencies directly to any nationwide employee or hiring manager in any form without a signed nationwide client services agreement on file and search engagement for that position will be deemed unsolicited in nature. no fee will be paid in the event the candidate is subsequently hired as a result of the referral or through other means.


nationwide pays on a geographic\-specific salary structure and placement within the actual starting salary range for this position will be determined by a number of factors including the skills, education, training, credentials and experience of the candidate; the scope, complexity and location of the role as well as the cost of labor in the market; and other conditions of employment. if a sales job, sales incentives, based on performance goals are possible in addition to this range. note on compensation for part\-time roles: please be aware that the salary ranges listed below reflect full\-time compensation. actual compensation may be prorated based on the number of hours worked relative to a full\-time schedule.

the expected starting salary range for engineer, software engineer : $60,900\.00 \- $91,300\.00

function development services experience engineering mobile digital emerging tech

  



cloud strategy hyperscalerfirst google cloud platform

  



level developer senior developer

java spring boot microservices primary focus



design and develop production grade microservices using java 1721 lts and spring boot 3x



implement core spring ecosystem components



ospring web spring webflux for reactive and nonreactive rest apis



ospring data jpa spring data r2dbc for database access



ospring security for oauth 20 jwt and rolebased access control



ospring cloud for service discovery config management and circuit breaking



apply domaindriven design ddd principles bounded contexts aggregates and domain events



implement cqrs command query responsibility segregation patterns for high performance read write separation



ensure high code quality through tdd unit testing junit 5 and integration testing test containers

design and implement graphql apis using spring for graphql or netflix dgs framework



define graphql schemas queries mutations and subscriptions for complex fmcg data models



implement dataloader patterns to solve n1 query problems in graphql resolvers



design federated graphql schemas for multiservice api composition



implement graphql security fieldlevel authorization query depth limiting and rate limiting



optimize graphql performance query complexity analysis persisted queries and response caching



event driven architecture primary focus



design and implement event driven architecture eda patterns



oevent sourcing capturing state changes as immutable event streams



osaga pattern managing distributed transactions across microservices



ooutbox pattern ensuring reliable event publishing with transactional consistency



ocqrs event sourcing combined patterns for high performance systems



build event producers and consumers using gcp pubsub and apache kafka



implement schema registry and avroprotobuf serialization for event contracts



design dead letter topics dlt and error handling strategies for event processing failures



ensure exactly once and atleast once delivery semantics based on business requirements



gcp cloud native implementation primary focus



deploy and manage microservices on gcp cloud run serverless and google kubernetes engine gke



implement gcpnative services



ogcp pubsub for asynchronous messaging and event streaming



ocloud sql postgresql and cloud spanner for transactional data



ofirestore for document oriented nosql workloads



ocloud memorystore redis for distributed caching



ocloud tasks for reliable async task execution



implement observability using gcp cloud logging cloud monitoring and cloud trace



manage secrets and configurations using gcp secret manager



api design integration



design and implement restful apis following openapi 30 specifications



build backendforfrontend bff services to optimize api responses for web and mobile clients



integrate microservices with enterprise systems



osap s4hana via odata and rfc adapters



osalesforce via rest api and platform events



oazure apim or apigee for api gateway and policy enforcement



implement api versioning strategies and backward compatibility patterns



senior developer responsibilities



lead the architectural design of microservices ecosystems and eventdriven platforms



define enterprise standards for java development api design and gcp resource governance



conduct rigorous architecture and code reviews across the team



mentor junior and midlevel developers in ddd eda and cloudnative patterns



drive performance engineering profiling jvm tuning and gcp resource optimization



collaborate with product owners architects and data engineers to define technical roadmaps

title: Senior DevOps Engineer (Kubernetes)
description:
role : senior ai native software engineer location: texas (remote \- occasionally travel only if needed) job type : 12\+ months contract (with possible extension) client is not looking any data scientist or data engineer . focus on someone who is 100% ai software engineer.


about the role we are seeking a hands\-on ai native software engineer to design, build, and deploy production\-grade ai\-driven systems within complex enterprise environments. in this role, you will focus on agent\-based architectures, ai platform integration, and cloud\-native development, delivering scalable, reliable solutions that power real business workflows. this is a 100% hands\-on engineering role, ideal for a senior technologist who thrives at the intersection of ai systems, software engineering, and cloud infrastructure.

design, implement, and maintain ai agent workflows, including retrieval\-augmented generation (rag), orchestration, tool/function invocation, and policy\-based routing build cloud\-native backend services and apis to support ai\-driven applications and enterprise integrations implement evaluation, monitoring, and observability frameworks to ensure accuracy, latency, reliability, and system health across ai agent lifecycles optimize ai and system performance across cost, scalability, and latency dimensions in production environments deliverables or project scope production\-ready ai\-powered applications aligned to defined business workflows and enterprise standards scalable multi\-model and multi\-provider ai architectures, including abstraction layers for provider flexibility fully deployed cloud\-native services using microservices, containers, and serverless or event\-driven patterns robust ci/cd pipelines, infrastructure\-as\-code implementations, logging, monitoring, and fault\-tolerant deployments

title: Senior Full Stack Engineer
description:
**about shyftoff**

shyftoff is reshaping how companies run contact center operations. our **agentic workforce delivery platform™** blends ai and human talent to help organizations scale their customer support with **speed, precision, and flexibility**. we’re building the future of **workforce orchestration** — where teams can launch, manage, and optimize an intelligent workforce that adapts to business needs in real time.

**why this role exists**

we’re growing rapidly and expanding the capabilities of our platform. to keep pace, we need a **hands\-on full stack engineer** who loves **building scalable systems**, **shipping high\-quality product**, and solving complex technical challenges that directly impact customers.

you’ll work across the **full stack** — from architecting backend services to building intuitive front\-end experiences — and collaborate closely with product and engineering leadership to bring ideas to life.

this is a role for someone who **takes ownership**, **moves with urgency**, and thrives in the energy of a **high\-growth startup**.

**what you’ll do**

**build \& ship product features**

* **design, build, and ship** features across the full stack with a focus on **performance, reliability, and scalability**.
* contribute to **architecture decisions** and help evolve the platform as we scale.
* write **clean, maintainable code** with thoughtful test coverage and documentation.

**engineering excellence \& execution**

* collaborate with engineering, product, and design to **translate requirements into technical solutions**.
* **break down complex problems** into clear scopes and deliver iteratively.
* participate in **code reviews**, share knowledge, and help raise the bar for engineering quality.

**platform \& infrastructure**

* work with **modern, cloud\-native infrastructure** and help strengthen our platform foundation as we grow.
* contribute to improving **developer tooling**, deployment workflows, and system observability.

**impact \& customer mindset**

* understand how customers use the platform and build with **usability, clarity, and automation** in mind.
* help **monitor product performance** and address issues before they impact users.

**you might be a fit if you…**

* have **3\+ years of experience shipping production saas applications**.
* are comfortable working **across the stack** — front\-end, back\-end, apis, and cloud.
* take pride in building **reliable, scalable systems** and feel **ownership** over what you ship.
* **thrive in a fast\-paced startup environment** where you move quickly, collaborate tightly, and embrace iteration.
* **communicate clearly** with engineers, product, and cross\-functional partners.

**bonus points if you have:**

* experience with **workforce management**, **contact center technology**, or **ai\-driven platforms**.
* built products for **operational teams** or workflow\-heavy environments.
* worked in a **high\-growth or early\-stage startup** (\<100 employees).

**our stack**

*(you don’t need to know everything here, but you should be comfortable diving in and learning quickly.)*

**frontend:** vue.js, react \+ next.js

**backend:** fastapi

**data**: postgresql

**cloud:** ecs, ec2, lambda, rds, s3

**tools:** n8n, cursor, github copilot, chatgpt

**why shyftoff**

this is a chance to join a **fast\-growing startup** building a **category\-defining product**. you’ll have the space to take **ownership**, influence how we scale, and see your work make an impact quickly.

feel free to apply here:

title: AI Analyst - Finance Technology
description:
**job description**

**victaulic** \| finance — global financial systems

**ai analyst — finance technology**


job description

**role at a glance**
====================

**job title**


ai analyst — finance technology

**department**


finance — global financial systems (gfs)

**reports to**


director, global financial systems

**location**


easton, pa (hybrid)

**experience**


3–5 years

**employment type**


full\-time

**position overview**
=====================


victaulic is seeking a skilled and motivated ai analyst to support finance technology automation within the global financial systems (gfs) team. this role sits at the intersection of finance operations, data engineering, and applied artificial intelligence, serving as a critical bridge between finance and it to translate emerging ai capabilities into practical, business\-ready solutions.


the ai analyst will work directly with gfs leadership to deliver measurable, data\-driven value to finance teams — including controllership, fp\&a, tax, treasury, ap, and ar — while expanding their own expertise in emerging ai disciplines. the role will train finance users on ai automation tools, and identify, document, scope, and build solutions in close collaboration with the it ai team, adhering to governance and security policies aligned with both it and finance standards.


this is a high\-visibility opportunity for a technically grounded professional familiar with finance operations and concepts, comfortable working with finance professionals at all levels to identify and develop automation solutions in close collaboration with other finance technology subject matter experts.


the ideal candidate combines hands\-on experience with agentic ai, generative ai, llms, python coding, and machine learning with a genuine curiosity about finance processes and a drive to transform how finance operates.

**key responsibilities**
========================

**finance value delivery**
--------------------------

* contribute to ai\-powered tools that address key finance challenges including close cycle optimization, forecasting accuracy, and reporting automation
* generate actionable insights for controllership, fp\&a, tax, treasury, ap, and ar teams
* orchestrate the transition from traditional finance reporting to dynamic, ai\-enabled reporting and analytics
* partner with finance stakeholders at all levels to identify processes ripe for ai\-driven transformation; document current\-state workflows, pain points, and requirements

**ai \& data bridge**
---------------------

* serve as a collaborative partner between the gfs team, finance operations, and it data teams
* translate finance data and platform outputs into ai\-ready formats and pipelines
* help define and execute on data requirements for finance ai use cases across close, consolidation, planning, forecasting, tax, and treasury

**generative \& agentic ai development**
----------------------------------------

* support deployment and iteration of generative ai and agentic ai use cases tailored to finance workflows
* author and maintain complex prompt libraries for claude, microsoft copilot, and chatgpt to support finance process automation, report generation, and intelligent document processing
* evaluate and test prompt effectiveness across tools and finance use cases
* design and build ai agent workflows that autonomously execute finance tasks such as data validation, reconciliation checks, variance analysis, and exception handling

**ai\-enabled reporting \& analytics**
--------------------------------------

* build ai\-powered dashboards, reports, and guided workflows
* leverage snowflake features including cortex ai for finance analytics and ai\-powered insights

**epm platform ai capabilities**
--------------------------------

* support the optimization of onestream ai forecasting, ai studio, agentic ai, and genesis capabilities within the epm platform
* collaborate with onestream developers to enhance platform capabilities, training materials, and documentation utilizing ai tools

**training, development \& governance**
---------------------------------------

* actively pursue skill growth in machine learning, ai governance, and advanced ai tooling
* contribute to ai governance practices, development of ai safeguards, and compliance with regulatory requirements (sox, gdpr, eu ai act)
* work closely with gfs leadership to develop enterprise standards based on best practice and ethical use of ai in finance, aligned with it governance
* document all models, processes, and solutions with clear technical and user\-facing documentation
* cross\-train with team resources to ensure business continuity

**required qualifications**
===========================

* 3–5 years of experience in data engineering, analytics engineering, ai development, or a closely related technical role
* solid foundational knowledge of ai technologies including generative ai, agentic ai, and machine learning (data science familiarity is preferred)
* demonstrated experience with agentic and generative ai tools (claude, chatgpt, copilot) in a professional setting
* proficiency in python coding for data manipulation, workflow development, and ai/ml model building
* proficiency in sql and experience with the snowflake data platform
* demonstrated ability to write complex, multi\-step prompts for llm tools
* experience supporting finance initiatives or working closely with finance teams on technology\-driven projects
* comfortable working with finance professionals at all levels — from analysts to senior leadership — to identify, document, and scope ai opportunities
* strong communication skills with the ability to collaborate across technical and non\-technical audiences

**skills at a glance**
======================

**ai / genai / agentic ai**  

required: solid working knowledge of ai, generative ai, and agentic ai concepts.  

preferred: hands\-on experience delivering ai or genai projects in a business environment.

**snowflake data platform**  

required: proficiency using the snowflake data platform.  

preferred: hands\-on experience implementing or operating snowflake in production environments.

**prompt engineering**  

required: familiarity using ai tools such as claude, copilot, and chatgpt.  

preferred: ability to work fluently across multiple ai platforms and models.

**python / sql**  

required: proficient in python and sql for data analysis and automation.  

preferred: experience writing and maintaining production\-level scripts and workflows.

**finance process \& operations knowledge**  

required: working understanding of finance processes and operations.  

preferred: deep domain expertise within finance functions.

**cross\-functional collaboration**  

required: ability to collaborate effectively with finance and it teams.  

preferred: experience operating at the program or portfolio level across functions.

**machine learning**  

required: foundational understanding of machine learning concepts.  

preferred: experience deploying or operationalizing machine learning models.

**data governance \& security**  

required: exposure to sox compliance, internal controls, or financial audit processes.  

preferred: not specified.

**financial systems platforms**  

required: exposure to finance systems and tools.  

preferred: experience with embedded ai capabilities in finance platforms such as onestream, m3, or alteryx.

**victaulic is an equal employment opportunity (eoe/m/f/vets/disabled) employer and welcomes all qualified applicants. applicants will receive fair and impartial consideration without regard to race, gender, color, religion, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, sexual orientation, genetic data, or other legally protected status. (background checks may be required as part of our pre\-employment process).**

*\#li\-kp1*

title: Data Scientist - DCCA
company: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
description:
**minimum education**  

  

bachelor's degree or equivalent experience  

  

**minimum experience**  

  

5  

  

**summary**  

  

conducts statistical and mathematical initiatives to predict future outcomes through the application of machine learning, natural language processing, and conceptual modeling. uses existing algorithms to test hypotheses through careful and deliberate model design. conducts statistical analysis, modeling, and simulation that leads to actionable decisions. applies statistical methods to characterize uncertainty using large, complex datasets. utilizes data mining techniques to optimize decisions that support division strategic objectives. transforms data into actionable information.

 **duties and responsibilities**

* responsible for the collection and refinement of data from multiple high\-volume data sources. gathers and transforms large volumes of data from internal and external systems in a manner suitable for analysis.
* works with large and complex data sets to solve difficult and non\-routine problems. performs data analysis in support of ad\-hoc and standing requests.
* participates on analytical research projects through all stages including concept formulation, determination of appropriate statistical methodology, data manipulation, research evaluation, and final research report.
* leverages large datasets while thinking strategically about uses of data. uses analytic models and works closely with cross\-functional teams and people to integrate solutions.
* conducts data studies and data discovery initiatives using existing and new data sources.
* performs data quality tests and suggests new methods to improve statistical inferences of variables across models.
* visualizes and reports data findings using a variety of formats to enhance insight into complex issues. communicates findings through internal reports and executive summaries.
* compiles, reviews, and assesses information from academic journals, market sources, and other reports to maintain state\-of\-the\-art knowledge in data analysis techniques.
**position requirements**
selected candidate will become part of dcca’s data analytics (da) team, which supports all aspects of the division’s mission to promote a fair and transparent financial services marketplace and strengthen communities for all consumers. da data scientists use a wide variety of financial, supervisory, community, economic, demographic, and geographic data to create multidimensional analytics and interactive data visualizations. the selected candidate will help to develop dcca’s ai program. along with other members of the da team, they will develop ai\-enabled tools leveraging generative ai technologies and modern coding assistants and identify opportunities to use automation and machine learning to improve operational effectiveness. candidates will be asked to participate in data analysis and writing exercises. **principal duties and responsibilities:*** design, build, and deploy interactive data science applications, dashboards, and analytical tools.
* develop, validate, and implement statistical and machine learning models to support analytical and policy\-related work using quantitative and qualitative data.
* apply ai and natural language processing methods, including large language model tools where appropriate, for tasks such as text analysis, classification, summarization, and information extraction.
* support data ingestion, transformation, validation, and maintenance of structured and unstructured data pipelines and analytical datasets.
* create clear visualizations, reports, and presentations to communicate findings to technical and non\-technical audiences.
* participate in analytical research projects and collaborate with staff across the division to identify, evaluate, and address complex business and policy questions.

 **knowledge/skill requirements:**
the ideal candidate is someone with strong problem\-solving, analytic and interpersonal skills, as well as a desire for continual learning.  

core technical skills* proficiency in python or r; experience with sql and data extraction, transformation, and pipeline development.
* knowledge of statistical methods, machine learning techniques, and model validation.
* experience developing analytical applications, dashboards, or apis and using software development best practices such as version control, testing, and documentation.
* experience with data visualization tools and libraries such as tableau, power bi, shiny, plotly, ggplot2, or matplotlib.
* experience with genai coding assistants and experience integrating llm apis into applications.
* knowledge of prompt engineering, rag systems, or llm fine\-tuning.
* familiarity with vector databases and semantic search.

  

professional skills* strong problem\-solving, critical thinking, and analytical abilities.
* strong written and verbal communication skills for technical and non\-technical audiences.
* ability to work independently, manage multiple priorities, and contribute to collaborative projects.
* dedication to continual learning and adapting to new tools and technologies.
* desire to innovate and proactively finds way to deliver results on new and complex projects.

  

domain knowledge* topical knowledge in consumer finance is preferred but not required. relevant experience includes:
* work with consumer financial data (credit reporting, mortgage, consumer loans)
* an understanding of financial services, banking regulations, or consumer protection
* work in a regulatory or policy\-oriented environment

  

grade 26 analysts:* must demonstrate knowledge and competence in the application of advanced theoretical and quantitative techniques in data science, statistics, mathematics, computer science, or other quantitative discipline typically achieved by completion of a bachelor’s degree plus five years of experience in applied statistical data analysis, survey management or equivalent combination of training and experience.
* completes assignments from senior management and official staff with considerable independence. reviews the work of others for technical soundness and accuracy.
* work output contributes to the planning and organizing of the section’s work, independently anticipating issues requiring further analysis.
* works with a high degree of independence in planning, organizing, and completion work products. ability to perform under time pressure and meet strict deadlines. excellent judgment and problem\-solving skills. ability to use known resources to seek answers and must be able to use initiative. develops solutions for data system issues and problems that do not have clear precedent.

  

grade 27 analysts:* must demonstrate knowledge and competence in the application of advanced theoretical and quantitative techniques in data science, statistics, mathematics, computer science, or other quantitative discipline typically achieved by completion of a bachelor’s degree plus six years of experience in applied statistical data analysis, survey management or equivalent combination of training and experience.
* completes assignments from senior management and official staff with considerable independence. reviews the work of others for technical soundness and accuracy.
* work output provides exceptional insight into large and sometimes messy datasets that determine the planning and organizing of the division’s work. creates solutions to complex issues and translates technical analysis and policy implications to senior staff, reserve bank staff, and the board.
* requires strong problem\-solving skills to identify and develop solutions to unique, unfamiliar issues. works with a high degree of independence in planning, organizing, and completion work products. performs under time pressure and meet strict deadlines. excellent judgment, problem\-solving skills, and uses initiative. develops solutions for data system issues and problems that do not have clear precedent.
* demonstrates organizational skills to identity priorities, drive performance\-based outcomes, and execute projects on schedule.

  

note:* this position is located in washington, dc, and requires in\-office presence.
* the expected salary range for this role is $128,600 \- $190,900, which spans all the posted grades. final offers are based on the grade for which you minimally qualify and are determined by experience and education, as well as internal and external factors.
  

* application deadline: friday, may 22, 2026, 11:59 pm et

title: Sr. Product Specialist, Analytics & Machine Learning
description:
**about northern trust:**


northern trust, a fortune 500 company, is a globally recognized, award\-winning financial institution that has been in continuous operation since 1889\.


northern trust is proud to provide innovative financial services and guidance to the world’s most successful individuals, families, and institutions by remaining true to our enduring principles of service, expertise, and integrity. with more than 130 years of financial experience and over 22,000 partners, we serve the world’s most sophisticated clients using leading technology and exceptional service.

**position overview**


northern trust asset management (ntam) is a global investment management firm with over a trillion dollars in assets under management, trusted by investors worldwide to help them navigate changing market environments and achieve their long\-term goals.


as part of our ongoing commitment to operational excellence and data\-driven decision\-making, we are seeking an experienced and highly skilled senior analytics specialist to join our asset management team. the ideal candidate will possess deep expertise in data science, artificial intelligence/machine learning (ai/ml), and a strong understanding of investment management processes.

**key responsibilities**
------------------------

* develop, implement, and maintain advanced analytics models using data science and ai/ml techniques to support portfolio management, investment operations, distribution and risk assessment.
* collaborate with investment professionals to identify analytical opportunities, translate business requirements into solutions, and communicate insights to stakeholders.
* analyze large and complex financial datasets to extract actionable insights and drive evidence\-based recommendations.
* build, validate, and optimize predictive models for driving growth, risk management, and operational efficiency.
* shepard the ai/ml models through various validation and governance committees.
* stay abreast of the latest trends in ai/ml, data engineering, and investment analytics, and proactively recommend innovative approaches to enhance the firm's analytical capabilities.
* partner with it and data engineering teams to ensure robust data pipelines, data integrity, and scalable analytics infrastructure.
* present complex analytical findings in a clear, concise, and compelling manner to both technical and non\-technical audiences, including senior management.
* mentor and guide junior analytics team members, fostering a culture of innovation, collaboration, and continuous learning.

**necessary qualifications \& experience**
------------------------------------------

* bachelor’s or master’s degree in data science, computer science, statistics, mathematics, finance, or a closely related field
* 7\+ years of hands\-on experience applying advanced data science and ai/ml techniques within asset management or investment management environments.
* strong understanding of portfolio management, asset allocation, and risk assessment practices, with a proven ability to translate investment concepts into analytical models.
* proficiency in programming languages and analytics tools such as python, r, sql, and modern machine learning frameworks (e.g., tensorflow, pytorch, scikit\-learn)
* hands on skills on platforms such as azure, snowflake, databricks and power bi
* experience working with large, complex financial datasets and leveraging data engineering best practices to ensure data quality and scalability.
* up\-to\-date knowledge of emerging trends and technologies in ai/ml, data engineering, and investment analytics.
* excellent communication skills, with the ability to present technical findings to both technical and non\-technical stakeholders, including senior management.
* leadership experience mentoring analytics professionals and fostering a collaborative, innovative team environment.


salary range:


$137,400 \- 233,600 usd*salary range is a good faith estimate of base pay. northern trust provides a comprehensive benefits package including retirement benefits (401k and pension), health and welfare benefits (medical, dental, vision, spending accounts and disability), paid time off, parental and caregiver leave, life \& accident insurance, and other voluntary and well\-being benefits. northern trust also provides a discretionary bonus program that may include an equity component.*

**working with us:**


as a northern trust partner, greater achievements await. you will be part of a flexible and collaborative work culture in an organization where financial strength and stability is an asset that emboldens us to explore new ideas.


movement within the organization is encouraged, senior leaders are accessible, and you can take pride in working for a company committed to assisting the communities we serve! join a workplace with a greater purpose.


we’d love to learn more about how your interests and experience could be a fit with one of the world’s most admired and sustainable companies! build your career with us and apply today. \#madeforgreater

**reasonable accommodation**

  



northern trust is committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to individuals with disabilities. if you need a reasonable accommodation for any part of the employment process, please email our hr service center at myhrhelp@ntrs.com.

  

  

we hope you’re excited about the role and the opportunity to work with us. we value an inclusive workplace and understand flexibility means different things to different people.


apply today and talk to us about your flexible working requirements and together we can achieve greater.

title: Systems Architect - Infrastructure
description:
analytica is seeking a **systems architect – infrastructure** to lead the design, implementation, and optimization of secure, scalable it infrastructure supporting advanced analytics and machine learning solutions. this role is ideal for a senior technical leader who can balance strategic infrastructure architecture with hands\-on execution.


the ideal candidate will play a critical role in ensuring infrastructure reliability, performance, and security, while enabling modern data and ai/ml workloads. working closely with cross\-functional technical leads, you will align infrastructure capabilities with program and mission requirements.


key responsibilities

* lead the design and architecture of scalable, resilient, and secure infrastructure environments
* oversee infrastructure planning, deployment, and ongoing maintenance across cloud and/or hybrid environments
* ensure high availability, performance, and reliability of systems supporting data, analytics, and machine learning workloads
* collaborate with data, application, and security teams to align infrastructure solutions with program requirements
* define and implement infrastructure standards, best practices, and architectural patterns
* monitor and optimize system performance, capacity, and cost efficiency
* ensure compliance with security, governance, and regulatory requirements
* support devsecops practices, including automation, ci/cd pipelines, and infrastructure as code (iac)
* troubleshoot complex infrastructure issues and provide root cause analysis
* mentor engineering teams and provide technical guidance on infrastructure\-related decisions

**required qualifications**

* experience in a senior infrastructure engineering or architecture role (level iii or equivalent)
* strong expertise in cloud platforms (aws, azure, or similar) and/or hybrid infrastructure environments
* experience designing and supporting infrastructure for data platforms, analytics, or machine learning workloads
* proficiency with infrastructure as code tools (e.g., terraform, cloudformation, or similar)
* strong understanding of networking, security, compute, and storage architectures
* experience with system monitoring, logging, and performance tuning
* familiarity with devsecops practices and ci/cd pipelines
* strong troubleshooting and problem\-solving skills

**preferred qualifications**

* experience supporting big data platforms (e.g., databricks, hadoop, spark)
* familiarity with containerization and orchestration technologies (e.g., docker, kubernetes)
* experience with security frameworks and compliance standards in regulated environments
* certifications such as aws certified solutions architect, aws certified devops engineer, or similar
* experience in federal or mission\-driven environments

  

about analytica: analytica is a leading consulting and information technology solutions provider to public sector organizations supporting health, civilian, and national security missions. founded in 2009 and headquartered in bethesda, md, the company is an established sba small business that has been recognized by inc. magazine each of the past three years as one of the 250 fastest\-growing companies in the u.s. analytica specializes in providing software and systems engineering, information management, analytics \& visualization, agile project management, and management consulting services. the company is appraised by the software engineering institute (sei) at cmmi® maturity level 3 and is an iso 9001:2008 certified provider.

  

analytica llc is an equal opportunity employer. we are committed to providing equal employment opportunities to all individuals, regardless of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, or any other characteristic protected by applicable federal, state, or local law. as a federal contractor, we comply with the vietnam era veterans' readjustment assistance act (vevraa) and take affirmative action to employ and advance in employment qualified protected veterans. we ensure that all employment decisions are based on merit, qualifications, and business needs. we prohibit discrimination and harassment of any kind. analytica llc also provides reasonable accommodations to applicants and employees with disabilities, in accordance with applicable law.
